findings.claim: The cpu.cores/cpu.sockets Optional+Computed carve-out question is raised and resolved (abandoned) consistently across ADR-004 and ADR-008, and the broader cpu/vga/rng conformance work is tracked as ongoing under issue #1231 without any ADR closing it out — three Accepted records (001-related framework migration umbrella, 004, and 008) each touch the same unresolved primitive of which VM sub-block fields should be Optional-only vs Optional+Computed, and none of them records the conformance work as done.

citations.quote: conformance — dropping `Computed` from all `cpu`/`vga`/`rng`/`memory` attributes — is tracked under [#1231](https://github.com/bpg/terraform-provider-proxmox/issues/1231). The originally-proposed `Optional+Computed` carve-out on `cpu.cores`/`cpu.sockets` was abandoned after empirical testing showed PVE does not auto-populate those keys on Read.
`cpu`/`vga`/`rng` schemas carry `Optional+Computed` on fields that should drop `Computed` per [ADR-004 §Provider Defaults vs PVE Defaults]

the `cpu.cores`/`cpu.sockets` candidates for this carve-out were empirically disproved (verified under [#1231](https://github.com/bpg/terraform-provider-proxmox/issues/1231)) and the `cpu` sub-package went Optional-only on all attributes.

findings.consequence: Maintainers reading only the ADR log would believe the cpu/vga/rng/memory Optional+Computed-to-Optional conformance is a settled, closed matter, when in fact both ADRs point to the same still-open tracking issue (#1231) for the actual code change; a reader might close out review of this area prematurely.
